diagrams.title: Servlet Lifecycle Timeline
diagrams.description: Horizontal timeline showing the three main phases of servlet lifecycle
elements: Arrows showing flow from left to right
Labels showing each phase happens at specific points
diagrams.purpose: Visualize that init() and destroy() are single events while service() is a continuous loop during the servlet's active life

diagrams.title: Thread Model in Servlets
diagrams.description: Diagram showing one servlet instance handling multiple concurrent client requests
elements: Single Servlet Instance box in center
Multiple Thread arrows (Thread 1, Thread 2, Thread n) entering servlet
Each thread has its own Stack (local variables)
Shared Heap area showing instance variables accessible by all threads
HttpServletRequest and HttpServletResponse objects for each request
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate why instance variables are not thread-safe while local variables are

diagrams.title: ServletConfig vs ServletContext
elements: Servlet A and Servlet B inside
ServletConfig box for each servlet (A and B) - their own parameters
ServletContext box spanning entire web application
Arrows showing ServletConfig points to specific servlet
Arrows showing ServletContext shared by all servlets
diagrams.purpose: Clarify that ServletConfig is per-servlet while ServletContext is application-wide
